title: Authenticated Scope, Secrets, SSRF, Untrusted Content, and Privacy · Changed-Input Lab
summary: An independent community information owner supplies a different origin, HTML structure, rate contract, accessibility need, resource ceiling, and injected failure and transfers authenticated scope, secrets, ssrf, untrusted content, and privacy into a changed least-authority secure crawl boundary. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of least-authority secure crawl boundary with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, cha…
objectives: Require explicit owner, account, role, purpose, paths, time, data, environment, and revocation authorization for sessions.
Acquire, scope, redact, rotate, destroy, and isolate secrets, cookies, CSRF material, caches, checkpoints, and reports per run and account.
Allow safe retrieval only, block state-changing forms, logout and destructive routes, and test unexpected GET side effects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Authenticated Authority
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go team handles case 5083 during a redirect, DNS, body, and checkpoint fault exercise. Build deterministic Go evidence to defend authenticated authority, secrets, request effects, output sinks, retention, and derived privacy state; every body, file, timer, transport, goroutine, channel, collector, and browser-service request needs one owner; then change one content version, metadata, accessibility, resource, browser-service, or report condition without overclaiming. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Require explicit owner, account, role, purpose, paths, time, data, environment, and revocation authorization for sessions.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Authenticated Authority.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for require explicit owner, account, role, purpose, paths, time, data, environment, and revocation authorization for sessions. For Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Authenticated Authority, keep Authenticated Authority connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Sensitive Retention
steps.instruction:

options.text: For test an acceptance boundary, crawl one convenient page, omit owner permission, revision, URL and robots policy, body and parser limits, record and graph provenance, frontier and goroutine ownership, Colly differences, changed fixtures, privacy, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as Go crawler proof.

Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.
